method_atom: a linear-response dielectric-function framework (random-phase approximation) for computing the collective self-sustained oscillation modes of an interacting many-body system of charged particles, together with their spatial confinement and their damping rates into single-particle (particle-hole) excitations and into quantized vibrational modes, using a number-conserving relaxation-time correction whose scattering time is fixed by an empirically measured transport relaxation time and whose coupling to the vibrational bath is inferred from its imprint on the response function
object_atom: plasmons (collective charge oscillations) in doped graphene at infra-red frequencies below the optical-phonon energy, whose losses arise from interband electron-hole pair creation, impurity collisions, and optical-phonon emission, examined for potential nanophotonic applications analogous to metal-dielectric surface plasmons
brief_reason: the RPA-plus-relaxation-time machinery for collective oscillation modes and their damping into single-particle and bosonic channels is transferable, while the doped-graphene Dirac electrons, their infra-red plasmons, and coupling to optical phonons are the domain-specific object.
